rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory section as concrete evidence that environmental cues increase product accessibility (the Halloween orange-cue example), which directly motivates the citing paper's cue-based WOM hypotheses. The citing paper then carries that logic into its own field experiment by manipulating cue-product links and testing effects on conversation, explicitly invoking the same accessibility mechanism. It is cited again in the implications section to connect cue-triggered accessibility to managerial actions, indicating an ongoing conceptual role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Berger and Fitzsimons (2008) by taking the environmental cue -> accessibility logic from product evaluation/choice to immediate and ongoing word-of-mouth outcomes. It builds on that prior cue-linking evidence to motivate and implement its own cue-based WOM tests.
